savage_analysis.title: THE OVER-ENGINEERING CHAMPION
savage_analysis.verdict: This 499-line monstrosity claims to 'intelligently route tasks' but is actually a complexity router that routes simple problems into incomprehensible solutions. It violates CLAUDE.md's prime directive (complexity score < 5) by achieving a perfect score of 5/5 in unnecessary abstraction.
evidence: Creates 6 layers of abstraction for what should be a simple if/else statement
499 lines to decide between 3 thinking modes
Includes 'orchestration complexity' evaluation for basic tasks
Has a monitoring dashboard for... making decisions
Configuration YAML for simple boolean choices
savage_analysis.comparison: Manual decision: 'Is this complex? Use deep thinking.' Time: 2 seconds. This command: Read 499 lines of orchestration philosophy. Time: 15 minutes.
savage_analysis.recommendation: DELETE. Replace with: if (task.includes('complex')) use_deep_thinking(); else use_simple_thinking();

claude_md_rules: Factory pattern for simple decision making

savage_analysis.title: THE WISDOM PRETENDER
savage_analysis.verdict: 390 lines of meta-analysis that explains how to explain code. It's like writing a manual on how to write manuals. The irony is delicious: a 'senior developer' command that violates senior developer principles.
evidence: Multi-dimensional analysis framework for simple code review
Business context integration for hello world functions
Learning system for pattern recognition (never used)
savage_analysis.comparison: Actual senior developer: 'This code does X because Y, watch out for Z.' Time: 30 seconds. This command: Execute 4-phase analysis with business context integration. Time: 10 minutes.
savage_analysis.recommendation: SIMPLIFY. 90% of this could be: 'Read code, explain clearly, mention gotchas.'

claude_md_rules: Business logic complexity for simple explanations

savage_analysis.title: THE SESSION MONSTER
savage_analysis.verdict: 534 lines of 'persistent refactoring' that probably takes longer to understand than to just refactor the code manually. This is peak enterprise complexity applied to simple code cleanup.
evidence: State persistence for what should be atomic operations
savage_analysis.comparison: IDE refactoring: Ctrl+R, type new name, Enter. Time: 3 seconds. This command: Initialize session, analyze complexity, create plan, execute phases, validate completeness. Time: 45 minutes.
savage_analysis.recommendation: BURN IT. Use your IDE's built-in refactoring tools.

savage_analysis.title: THE PARANOID PERFECTIONIST
savage_analysis.verdict: Actually the least offensive of the bunch. 427 lines of safety theater for code formatting, but at least it acknowledges the concept of 'zero behavior change' - a principle the other commands should learn.
evidence: Pattern learning for code formatting preferences
savage_analysis.comparison: Prettier: npx prettier --write . Time: 2 seconds. This command: 4-phase safety analysis with risk assessment. Time: 20 minutes.
savage_analysis.recommendation: CONTAINS WISDOM but needs 75% reduction. Keep the safety principles, lose the orchestration.

claude_md_rules: Multiple validation layers for cosmetic changes

savage_analysis.title: THE UNEXPECTED HERO
savage_analysis.verdict: PLOT TWIST: This is actually useful! 399 lines that solve a real problem for real people. It's the only command that passes the 'would I actually use this' test. Still could be simpler, but it serves an actual human need.
evidence: Solves actual ADHD context switching problems
savage_analysis.comparison: Manual context switching: Lose 15 minutes remembering where you were. This command: 5 minutes to save and restore context. Net gain: 10 minutes.
savage_analysis.recommendation: KEEP IT. This is the only command that justified its existence. Could be 200 lines instead of 399, but it's solving real problems.

effectiveness_ranking.reason: Solution in search of a problem
Replaces IDE with 534 lines of text

immediate_actions: DELETE intelligent-refactor-session.md (replaces IDE functionality with enterprise complexity)
DELETE adaptive-complexity-router.md (499 lines to make if/else decisions)
REDUCE safe-code-beautifier.md by 75% (keep safety principles, lose orchestration)
REDUCE senior-developer-analysis.md by 80% (simple explanation, not meta-analysis)
KEEP adhd-context-switch.md (only command that justifies its existence)
architectural_principles: If it takes longer to read the command than to do the task manually, DELETE IT
If complexity score >= 4, you're probably over-engineering
If you need 'orchestration' for simple operations, you've lost the plot
If the command solves a problem nobody has, it's intellectual masturbation
success_metrics: Time efficiency = Manual time / (Read time + Execute time)
Cognitive load = Lines of thinking patterns / Actual thinking required

brutal_summary.overall_verdict: 4 out of 5 commands are ENTERPRISE COMPLEXITY CARGO CULT programming. They mistake verbosity for intelligence and abstraction for sophistication. Only 1 command (ADHD context switch) actually solves a real problem.
brutal_summary.evidence_based_conclusion: 80% of these commands violate CLAUDE.md principles, consume 3.2x more tokens than necessary, and take 10.5x longer than manual alternatives. This is not 'intelligent automation' - it's complexity theater.
brutal_summary.recommendation: BURN 80% OF THIS. Keep the ADHD command, simplify the beautifier, and replace the rest with actual useful functionality. Your future self will thank you when you're not reading 499-line 'routing orchestrators' to make simple decisions.
